supply_chain.dependencies: Hardware systems operate on various platforms with remarketing agreements with IBM and other hardware providers
risks: Failures of third-party service providers could lead to financial loss
Reliance on third-party vendors for hosting environments increases as computing moves out of data centers
Vendor concentration risk as certain third-party vendors provide large portions of hosting needs
Third-party vendors subject to cybersecurity incidents, operational disruptions, hardware/software defects, human error, power losses, natural disasters

regulatory_risks: Extensive federal and state regulation of financial services industry
Federal truth-in-lending and truth-in-savings rules compliance
Privacy of Consumer Financial Information regulations
Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act compliance
Subject to examination by OCC, Federal Reserve Board, FDIC, NCUA, CFPB
Bank Service Company Act examination requirements
Payment Card Data Security Standards compliance
Card association and network compliance rules (Visa, MasterCard, Zelle, FedNow, RTP)
State privacy and cybersecurity laws (New York DFS cybersecurity requirements, California Consumer Privacy Act, Iowa Consumer Data Protection Act, Virginia Consumer Data Protection Act)
Fragmented state-by-state data privacy requirements creating compliance complexity
Evolving AI and machine learning regulations at state and federal levels
Potential changes to existing regulations or new regulations requiring costly programming changes
Regulatory actions could influence client purchasing decisions and timing
